## 7. AI Workflows & Tactics
- Date: 2026-04-12. Link: [Google AI Edge Eloquent](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/google-ai-edge-eloquent/id6756505519). Summary: The only clearly actionable workflow surfaced in the source set is Google's on-device dictation app, which uses Gemma-based models to turn rough speech into polished text offline. Setup: install the app, let it download the local models, keep offline mode on if privacy matters, dictate, then choose output modes like `Key points`, `Formal`, `Short`, or `Long` and paste the clipboard result into mail, notes, or chat. Available now: Yes, on iPhone. Why it matters: This is a real edge-AI workflow available now, useful where latency, privacy, or unreliable connectivity rule out cloud transcription.

## 9. Podcast Highlights
Source: [AI Daily Brief - Anthropic Now Leads OpenAI in Annualized Revenue](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iRMIUmDZrtY)

- Date: 2026-04-12. Links: [Anthropic compute announcement](https://www.anthropic.com/news/google-broadcom-partnership-compute), [Anthropic Series G note](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-raises-30-billion-series-g-funding-380-billion-post-money-valuation). Summary: The episode's lead theme was that Anthropic has moved into a new scale bracket: over $30B run-rate revenue and a multi-gigawatt TPU expansion with Google and Broadcom. Available now: Claude is live now; the added TPU capacity is expected from 2027. Use: This is not a new end-user tool; operators use Claude through Anthropic or cloud partners as before, while the new deal mainly changes supply assurance. Why it matters: The frontier race is increasingly constrained by secured power and compute, not just model quality.

- Date: 2026-04-12. Links: [Meta Muse Spark announcement](https://about.fb.com/news/2026/04/introducing-muse-spark-meta-superintelligence-labs/), [Axios on Meta's planned open-source variants](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/06/meta-open-source-ai-models). Summary: The episode highlighted Meta's shift toward a hybrid model posture: Muse Spark is closed at launch, while Meta still signals future open-source versions of some next models. Available now: Muse Spark is live in the Meta AI app and website, with broader rollout and limited partner API preview. Use: Test it through Meta AI today; developer access remains constrained. Why it matters: Meta appears to be moving from pure open-weight signaling toward a mixed deployment strategy that protects its strongest systems while keeping ecosystem reach.

- Date: 2026-04-12. Link: [Forbes on "token maxing"](https://www.forbes.com/sites/richardnieva/2026/03/31/the-ai-gods-spending-as-much-as-they-can-on-ai-tokens/). Summary: The episode treated "token maxing" as a live management signal inside AI-heavy engineering orgs: token consumption is becoming a proxy for AI adoption and, in some teams, perceived productivity. Available now: Yes, as an operating practice, not a product. Use: If adopted internally, pair token-spend tracking with delivery, defect, and review metrics; spend alone is a weak KPI. Why it matters: This is an early indicator that model usage cost is becoming part of labor management and engineering culture, not just infra accounting.
